title: Project Spotlight: PostgreSQL — The Database Community Ownership Kept Independent
excerpt: How PostgreSQL grew from a Berkeley research database into a community-governed project used across the industry.

title: Project Spotlight: Nginx — The Web Server Built to Solve the C10K Problem
excerpt: Why Nginx's event-driven architecture made it the default choice for serving high-traffic websites.

title: Project Spotlight: FFmpeg — The Multimedia Toolkit Almost Everything Quietly Depends On
excerpt: FFmpeg's audio and video libraries sit invisibly inside browsers, streaming platforms, and editing tools.

title: Project Spotlight: Godot Engine — A Community-Owned Alternative in Game Development
excerpt: How Godot's MIT license and nonprofit backing set it apart from commercial game engines.

title: Project Spotlight: Redis — What Its License Reversal Says About Trust
excerpt: Redis moved away from open source licensing in 2024, then reversed course in 2025 — a case study in what happens when a project's license and its community disagree.

title: Project Spotlight: Django — The Framework a Newsroom Built and Gave Away
excerpt: Django started as an internal tool at a newspaper before becoming one of the web's most widely used frameworks.

title: Project Spotlight: Home Assistant — Local-First Smart Home Software Under Foundation Ownership
excerpt: Home Assistant's ownership moved to a nonprofit foundation in 2024 to protect its local-first, privacy-focused approach.

title: Project Spotlight: Ansible — Agentless Automation That Changed Configuration Management
excerpt: Ansible popularized agentless, YAML-based infrastructure automation and went on to be acquired by Red Hat.

title: Project Spotlight: Visual Studio Code — An Editor Bet That Reshaped Developer Tooling
excerpt: Built on Electron and released by Microsoft in 2015, VS Code's extension ecosystem made it one of the most widely used code editors.

title: Project Spotlight: PyTorch — From a Single Company's Research Lab to Foundation Governance
excerpt: PyTorch moved from Meta's sole ownership to a dedicated foundation under the Linux Foundation in 2022.

title: Project Spotlight: Node.js — Bringing JavaScript Out of the Browser
excerpt: Node.js paired JavaScript with Chrome's V8 engine to make server-side JavaScript practical at scale.

title: Project Spotlight: Vue.js — A Framework Sustained by Sponsorship, Not a Company
excerpt: Vue grew from one developer's side project into a major framework funded largely through community sponsorship.

title: Project Spotlight: Rust — Memory Safety Without a Garbage Collector
excerpt: Rust grew from a Mozilla research project into an independently governed language used across systems programming.

title: Project Spotlight: React — How a Facebook Side Project Became the Web's Default UI Library
excerpt: React's component model and virtual DOM reshaped how much of the industry builds interactive interfaces.

title: Project Spotlight: Kubernetes — The Container Orchestrator That Became the Default
excerpt: Donated by Google to the Cloud Native Computing Foundation in 2015, Kubernetes became the standard way to run containers at scale.

title: Project Spotlight: The Linux Kernel — Three Decades of Collaborative Engineering
excerpt: Started by Linus Torvalds in 1991, the Linux kernel now runs everything from smartphones to the world's fastest supercomputers.
